# x402

> HTTP 402 payment server. AI agents pay native USDC on Base via EIP-3009 `transferWithAuthorization` in exchange for a Sentinel decentralized VPN subscription. One HTTP request, one signature, zero prerequisites — no custom contract, no `approve()`, no bridge, no KYC.

The agent signs an EIP-3009 message off-chain. The self-hosted facilitator settles USDC on Base (~2s). The server provisions a Sentinel subscription share + a fee grant in one atomic TX. The agent then opens a direct WireGuard/V2Ray tunnel to a node. The operator never sees the tunnel or the traffic.

## Prerequisites

The agent needs exactly one thing before calling x402: an EVM private key with at least $1.00 USDC on Base (chainId 8453). Everything else — Sentinel wallet, P2P gas, node selection, tunnel keys — is provisioned by x402 or generated locally by `blue-js-sdk/ai-path`.

**Payment is identical on every platform.** What differs is *connecting* the tunnel after the server returns 200. There are two equally-supported connect paths — pick by OS:

- **macOS & Linux:** Use the native `sentinel-dvpncli` (one `go install`, Go 1.24+), then drive it with three flag-only commands — see **"macOS & Linux — native CLI connect"** below. The CLI does NOT bundle a tunnel: it shells out to `wg-quick` for WireGuard nodes, so install **wireguard-tools** first (`apt install wireguard-tools` | `brew install wireguard-tools` | `pacman -S wireguard-tools`), or a `v2ray` binary on PATH for V2Ray nodes. Most nodes are WireGuard; bringing the interface up needs sudo. **Only exception: Fedora** — its SELinux blocks VPN interfaces and cannot be worked around programmatically; macOS, Ubuntu, Debian and Arch are fine once the tunnel binary is installed.
- **Windows (zero prerequisites):** pass `protocol: 'v2ray'` to `connect()` and you need **no admin and no manual install**. V2Ray is a userspace SOCKS5 proxy; `connect()` auto-downloads the `v2ray` binary to `~/.sentinel-sdk/bin` on first use (no admin) and covers ~70% of the network (~630 nodes). That is the complete Windows path: `npm install` → pay → `connect({ ..., protocol: 'v2ray' })`. WireGuard nodes (the other ~30%, faster) are an **optional admin-once upgrade** — the MSI install and tunnel service need elevation (`cscript run-admin.vbs setup.js` triggers one UAC prompt; a service running as SYSTEM/admin needs none). The SDK checks admin **before payment** and refuses a WireGuard node when not elevated, so no USDC or P2P is ever wasted. If you cannot elevate, stay on `protocol: 'v2ray'`. `await setup()` is optional (it pre-fetches V2Ray; `connect()` fetches it anyway). See **"Windows — JS-SDK connect"** below.

Both paths consume the exact same 200 response (`subscriptionId`, `planId`, `feeGranter`, `nodeAddress`) and end in the same `MsgStartSession` + direct WireGuard/V2Ray handshake.

How to fund a fresh EVM key with USDC on Base:
- **Coinbase / Coinbase CDP**: withdraw USDC and pick "Base" as the network. ~30 seconds, no bridge fee.
- **Any CEX that supports Base withdrawals** (Binance, Kraken, OKX, Bybit): same flow.
- **Bridge from Ethereum L1**: use `bridge.base.org` (canonical bridge) or any third-party bridge (Across, Stargate). USDC on Base is native after Circle's CCTP — no wrapped tokens.
- **DEX swap on Base**: if the wallet already holds ETH on Base, swap to USDC on Uniswap or Aerodrome.

Confirm with: `curl https://api.basescan.org/api?module=account&action=tokenbalance&contractaddress=0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913&address=<your_addr>`

No ETH is required for the agent's wallet — EIP-3009 `transferWithAuthorization` is gasless from the agent's side (the facilitator pays Base gas).

## Live endpoints

Base URL: `https://x402.sentinel.co`

Paid (HTTP 402, body `{ "sentinelAddr": "sent1...", "country": "DE" }` — `country` optional):
- POST `/vpn/connect/1day` — $0.033 USDC, 1 day
- POST `/vpn/connect/7days` — $0.233 USDC, 7 days
- POST `/vpn/connect/30days` — $1.00 USDC, 30 days

Free:
- GET `/manifest` — full machine-readable protocol spec (use this first, ~300 tokens)
- GET `/pricing` — human pricing table
- GET `/nodes` — VPN nodes in operator plan with live geo data (country, city, protocol, online) and a `byCountry` summary
- GET `/health` — uptime
- GET `/agent/:sentinelAddr` — subscription + fee-grant status

## Payment

- Network: Base mainnet (`eip155:8453`, chainId 8453)
- Asset: USDC at `0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913` (6 decimals)
- Scheme: `exact` / EIP-3009 `transferWithAuthorization`
- Gasless for agent on Base settlement (facilitator pays)
- Gasless for agent on Sentinel session start (operator fee-grants)

## Agent flow

1. POST to a paid endpoint with `{ sentinelAddr }` (add `country` to request a node location — validated before payment).
2. Server returns 402 with `PAYMENT-REQUIRED` header (amount, asset, payTo, network).
3. Agent signs EIP-3009 locally (EIP-712).
4. Agent resends with `PAYMENT-SIGNATURE` header — `@x402/fetch` does this automatically.
5. Facilitator settles USDC on Base.
6. Server submits atomic `MsgShareSubscription` + `MsgGrantAllowance` on Sentinel.
7. Server returns 200 `{ provisioned, subscriptionId, planId, feeGranter, nodeAddress, nodeCountry, nodes[], sentinelTxHash, expiresAt }`.
8. Agent connects, using the path for its OS (both consume the same 200 fields):
   - **macOS/Linux:** import the key (`printf '%s\n\n' "$MNEMONIC" | sentinel-dvpncli keys add agent --keyring.backend test`), `sentinel-dvpncli tx session-start <nodeAddress> --subscription-id <subscriptionId> --tx.fee-granter-addr <feeGranter> --tx.from-name agent --keyring.backend test`, resolve the new id with `sentinel-dvpncli query sessions --account-addr <sent1...> --subscription-id <subscriptionId> --output-format json` (newest entry), then `sentinel-dvpncli connect <sessionId>`. (See "macOS & Linux — native CLI connect".)
   - **Windows:** `connect({ mnemonic, subscriptionId, feeGranter, nodeAddress, protocol: 'v2ray' })` — zero admin, zero manual install (the v2ray binary auto-downloads). Omit `protocol` only if you have admin and want WireGuard nodes too. (See "Windows — JS-SDK connect".)
9. `MsgStartSession` on Sentinel — gas paid by `feeGranter` (the fee grant the server created in step 6, so the agent's session start costs zero P2P).
10. Direct WireGuard/V2Ray handshake — tunnel up. The operator never sees it.

## Reference code — payment (all platforms) + Windows connect

The payment half of this snippet is identical on every OS. The trailing `connect()` is the
Windows path (JS-SDK tunnel). On macOS/Linux, stop after you have `provision` and switch to the
CLI recipe in the next section — the request/response is byte-for-byte the same.

```typescript
import { x402Client, wrapFetchWithPayment } from '@x402/fetch';
import { ExactEvmScheme } from '@x402/evm/exact/client';
import {
  setup, createWallet, connect,
  rpcQueryNodesForPlan, createRpcQueryClientWithFallback,
} from 'blue-js-sdk/ai-path';

await setup(); // optional: pre-fetch V2Ray (no admin). connect({ protocol: 'v2ray' }) auto-fetches it anyway.
const wallet = await createWallet();
const scheme = new ExactEvmScheme({ address, signTypedData });
const client = new x402Client();
client.register('eip155:8453', scheme);
const paidFetch = wrapFetchWithPayment(fetch, client);

const res = await paidFetch('https://x402.sentinel.co/vpn/connect/30days', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({ sentinelAddr: wallet.address, country: 'DE' }), // country optional
});
const provision = await res.json();
const { subscriptionId, feeGranter, planId } = provision;

// If the server didn't pre-select a node, query the plan directly
let nodeAddress = provision.nodeAddress;
if (!nodeAddress) {
  const rpc = await createRpcQueryClientWithFallback();
  const { items } = await rpcQueryNodesForPlan(rpc, planId, { status: 1, limit: 50 });
  nodeAddress = items[0]?.address;
}

const vpn = await connect({
  mnemonic: wallet.mnemonic,
  subscriptionId: String(subscriptionId),
  feeGranter,
  nodeAddress,
  protocol: 'v2ray', // zero admin, zero manual install — Windows zero-prereq path.
                     // Omit only if you have admin and want WireGuard nodes (faster, ~30% of network).
});
// vpn => { connected, ip, protocol, sessionId, nodeAddress, socksPort }
// vpn.ip is the exit IP, verified THROUGH the tunnel by the SDK.

// IMPORTANT (v2ray only): protocol: 'v2ray' is a userspace SOCKS5 proxy, NOT a system route.
// A plain fetch() still exits on your real IP — that's expected, not a failure (vpn.ip already
// proves routing). Send your traffic through vpn.socksPort with a SOCKS agent, or use
// protocol: 'wireguard' for a transparent full-system route. socksPort is DYNAMIC — read it
// from the connect() result, never hardcode.
import { SocksProxyAgent } from 'socks-proxy-agent';
const proxy = new SocksProxyAgent(`socks5h://127.0.0.1:${vpn.socksPort}`);
const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/sensitive', { dispatcher: proxy }); // exits on vpn.ip
```

## macOS & Linux — native CLI connect

After the `paidFetch` call above returns `provision`
(`{ subscriptionId, planId, feeGranter, nodeAddress, ... }`), do NOT call the JS `connect()`.
Instead drive Sentinel's native `sentinel-dvpncli` — three fully flag-driven commands, no config
files, no interactive prompts when you pass the flags below.

Unlike the Windows path, the native CLI does NOT bundle a tunnel — it shells out to the system
binary on your PATH. WireGuard nodes run `wg-quick`, so you must install **wireguard-tools**
(`apt install wireguard-tools` | `brew install wireguard-tools` | `pacman -S wireguard-tools`).
V2Ray nodes run `v2ray`, so if you select a V2Ray node you also need a **v2ray** binary on PATH.
Most nodes are WireGuard, so install wireguard-tools at minimum. Bringing the interface up needs sudo.

**Install once (Go 1.24+):**

```bash
go install github.com/sentinel-official/sentinel-dvpncli@latest   # binary lands in $(go env GOPATH)/bin
# or from source: git clone https://github.com/sentinel-official/sentinel-dvpncli && cd sentinel-dvpncli && make install
sentinel-dvpncli version
```

**Connect (drive these with the fields from `provision`):**

```bash
# 1. Import the SAME wallet whose sentinelAddr (sent1...) you sent to x402.
#    `keys add` is INTERACTIVE — there is NO --recover flag. It prompts for the mnemonic,
#    then a BIP-39 passphrase. Feed BOTH from stdin: line 1 = mnemonic, blank line 2 = default
#    (empty) passphrase. A single-line echo would hang on the passphrase prompt.
#    --keyring.backend test makes it non-interactive for the OS keychain (no keychain prompt).
printf '%s\n\n' "$AGENT_MNEMONIC" | sentinel-dvpncli keys add agent --keyring.backend test
#    (keyring.backend defaults to "os"; use "test" or "file" for scripted/agent use.)

# 2. Start the session against the subscription x402 shared with you.
#    --tx.fee-granter-addr = provision.feeGranter  => the operator pays the P2P gas, you pay zero.
#    Positional arg          = provision.nodeAddress (sentnode1...).
sentinel-dvpncli tx session-start "$NODE_ADDRESS" \
  --subscription-id "$SUBSCRIPTION_ID" \
  --tx.fee-granter-addr "$FEE_GRANTER" \
  --tx.from-name agent \
  --keyring.backend test \
  --rpc.chain-id sentinelhub-2 \
  --output-format json

# 3. Resolve the session id you just created. `tx session-start` prints {response, result}
#    but does NOT surface the new id as a clean field, so query it back: filter sessions by
#    YOUR account + the subscription and take the newest. AGENT_ADDR = your sent1... address.
SESSION_ID=$(sentinel-dvpncli query sessions \
  --account-addr "$AGENT_ADDR" --subscription-id "$SUBSCRIPTION_ID" \
  --output-format json | jq '.result[-1].id // .response[-1].id')

# 4. Bring the tunnel up. Takes the SESSION id (not the subscription id). No key needed —
#    connect only queries the session on-chain and builds the tunnel.
#    Requires wg-quick on PATH (install wireguard-tools) and sudo to create the interface.
sentinel-dvpncli connect "$SESSION_ID"
```

Defaults baked into the CLI (override only if needed): `--rpc.addrs https://rpc.sentinel.co:443`,
`--rpc.chain-id sentinelhub-2`, `--keyring.name sentinel`. Useful extras: `sentinel-dvpncli keys list`,
`sentinel-dvpncli query ...` (sessions/subscriptions/nodes), `sentinel-dvpncli inspect`,
`sentinel-dvpncli tx session-cancel <id>` to tear down. `connect` traps SIGINT/SIGTERM and runs a
clean down sequence on exit.

> **Fedora caveat:** Fedora's SELinux blocks VPN interfaces and an agent cannot bypass it. This is
> the only OS x402 cannot connect on. macOS, Ubuntu, Debian and Arch all work once you install the
> tunnel binary the CLI shells out to (wireguard-tools, plus v2ray for V2Ray nodes) — see above.

## Autonomous flow — separate site

There is a second, fully self-funded path onto Sentinel where the agent holds its own P2P, picks
any node, and pays it directly — no operator, no fee-grant, no subscription.
That path lives on its own dedicated site and is NOT served by x402. If you want zero
intermediaries (and are willing to hold P2P + pay your own gas), read the autonomous guide and its
agent-readable summary instead: https://autonomous.sentinel.co — https://autonomous.sentinel.co/llms.txt

## Packages

- `@x402/fetch` — wraps `fetch`, auto-signs 402 responses
- `@x402/evm` — `ExactEvmScheme` EIP-3009 signer
- `blue-js-sdk/ai-path` — Windows connect path: environment setup (`setup` — auto-installs tunnel binaries), Sentinel wallet (`createWallet`, `importWallet`), tunnel (`connect`, `disconnect`), and plan/node discovery (`rpcQueryNodesForPlan`, `createRpcQueryClientWithFallback`) — WireGuard/V2Ray
- `sentinel-dvpncli` — macOS/Linux connect path: native Go CLI (`go install github.com/sentinel-official/sentinel-dvpncli@latest`, needs Go 1.24+). Flag-driven `keys` / `tx session-start` / `connect` — drives the tunnel without the JS SDK. Does NOT bundle a tunnel: shells out to `wg-quick` (install wireguard-tools) or `v2ray` on PATH.

## Request schema

```json
{ "sentinelAddr": "sent1...", "country": "DE" }
```

`sentinelAddr` matches `^sent1[0-9a-z]{38}$` (Sentinel bech32). Generate with `createWallet()` from `blue-js-sdk/ai-path`.

`country` is optional — ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code (`DE`) or name (`Germany`). If no online plan node matches, the server returns `COUNTRY_UNAVAILABLE` with `availableCountries` **before** payment. Check `GET /nodes` for the live `byCountry` summary.

## Response schema (200)

```json
{
  "provisioned": true,
  "subscriptionId": 1192288,
  "planId": 42,
  "feeGranter": "sent1...",
  "nodeAddress": "sentnode1...",
  "nodeCountry": "Germany",
  "nodes": ["sentnode1a...", "sentnode1b..."],
  "sentinelTxHash": "...",
  "expiresAt": "2026-06-17T00:00:00Z"
}
```

## Errors

All errors are JSON `{ code, message, nextAction, docs }`. Branch on `code` — it is the stable contract. The codes marked pre-payment are returned **before** payment is taken. Beyond that, USDC settlement only happens after a successful 2xx response — error responses (including `PROVISIONING_FAILED`) never charge the agent.

| `code` | Status | Pre-payment | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MISSING_SENTINEL_ADDR` | 400 | yes | Body did not include `sentinelAddr`. |
| `INVALID_SENTINEL_ADDR` | 400 | yes | `sentinelAddr` does not match `^sent1[0-9a-z]{38}$`. Use `createWallet()` from `blue-js-sdk/ai-path`. |
| `UNKNOWN_TIER` | 404 | yes | Path tier is not `1day` \| `7days` \| `30days`. |
| `COUNTRY_UNAVAILABLE` | 400 | yes | Requested `country` has no online plan node. Response includes `availableCountries` — resend with one of those or omit `country`. |
| `CAPACITY_EXHAUSTED` | 503 | yes | Operator cannot provision right now (subscription pool full, operator balance below new-subscription cost). Retry later — re-checked every 60s. |
| `PAYMENT_REQUIRED` | 402 | — | Sign EIP-3009 `transferWithAuthorization` and resend with the `PAYMENT-SIGNATURE` header. `@x402/fetch` handles this automatically. |
| `PROVISIONING_FAILED` | 500 | — | Sentinel TX failed after payment verification. USDC was **not** charged (settlement only happens on 2xx). Safe to retry. |
| `NOT_FOUND` | 404 | — | No route matches. See `/manifest`. |
| `INTERNAL_ERROR` | 500 | — | Unexpected server error. |

## Security properties

- Agent's Sentinel private key never leaves the agent. Handshake is agent ↔ node.
- Agent's EVM key never leaves the agent. Only the EIP-3009 signature is transmitted.
- Self-hosted facilitator option — no third party in the payment path.
- Every settlement is a verifiable public Base transaction.
- Operator stores `sentinelAddr` only. No IPs, traffic, or logs.
- Tunnel is end-to-end encrypted (WireGuard or V2Ray).

## Sentinel chain endpoints

`blue-js-sdk` ships verified RPC and LCD fallbacks and rotates through them automatically (`createRpcQueryClientWithFallback()`); add more at runtime with `addRpcEndpoint(url)`.

- RPC: `rpc.sentinel.co:443`, `sentinel-rpc.polkachu.com`, `rpc.mathnodes.com`, `sentinel-rpc.publicnode.com`, `rpc.sentinel.quokkastake.io`, `rpc.sentinel.suchnode.net` (additional public endpoint, suchnode.net)
- LCD (fallback only): `lcd.sentinel.co`, `sentinel-api.polkachu.com`, `api.sentinel.quokkastake.io`, `sentinel-rest.publicnode.com`, `api.sentinel.suchnode.net`

## Quick discovery for agents

Hit `GET /manifest` first. It returns the entire protocol — endpoints, pricing in atomic units, request/response schemas, the state machine, and a runnable code example — as JSON in ~300 tokens.

- README: https://github.com/Sentinel-Bluebuilder/x402/blob/master/README.md
- Manifesto: https://github.com/Sentinel-Bluebuilder/x402/blob/master/MANIFESTO.md
- x402 spec: https://github.com/coinbase/x402
- Sentinel: https://sentinel.co
